snapshotSource: git status --short; git diff --stat; git diff --name-only; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard

categories.label: A. package dependency changes
categories.recommendedAction: Run a dedicated dependency audit, inspect package.json and package-lock.json together, then commit only after dependency intent is confirmed.

categories.label: B. Prisma/schema/migration changes
categories.recommendedAction: Run a dedicated Prisma audit. Do not auto-commit and do not run migration until schema intent, migration files, backup evidence, and explicit approval are present.

categories.label: C. admin UI / admin route changes
categories.recommendedAction: Review authorization, admin behavior, and UI scope in a separate admin batch.

categories.label: D. API route changes
categories.recommendedAction: Review API behavior, auth, request validation, and compatibility in a separate API batch.

categories.label: E. core business logic changes
categories.recommendedAction: Separate src/lib non-EBOS changes by domain such as SEO, analytics, storage, media, license generation, settings, and localization before any commit.

categories.label: F. EBOS report residue
categories.recommendedAction: Can be committed later as a dedicated report-only batch after confirming paths stay under reports/ebos/** and contain no secrets.

categories.label: G. generated/cache/build files
categories.recommendedAction: Exclude from commit. Clean only with explicit user confirmation because this step must not delete files.

categories.label: H. unknown/unclassified files
categories.recommendedAction: Triage by source and intent before staging. Do not mix with package, Prisma, admin, API, or EBOS report commits.

recommendedNextSteps: Do not use git add . on the current worktree.
Audit package.json and package-lock.json in a dedicated dependency batch.
Audit all prisma/** files separately and do not run migration without explicit approval.
Audit admin and API changes separately with auth and behavior checks.
Split src/lib non-EBOS changes by business domain before any commit.
Commit reports/ebos/** residue only as a dedicated report-only batch after secret/path review.
Prepare a clean Promotion Manager development branch only after current dirty ownership is resolved.
warnings: Short status has 100 untracked entries, while git ls-files --others expands those directories to 369 untracked files.
The worktree still contains package, Prisma, admin, API, core src/lib, generated/cache, and unknown file groups.
This audit intentionally did not stage, commit, push, delete, revert, deploy, or run migration.
blockers: Remaining dirty files are too broad to commit together safely.
Unknown/unclassified files require owner and intent triage before staging.
Prisma and package changes require separate approval before any commit.
